name: Service Move: Shutoff at Old + Start at New Without Overlap Charges
description: Call a utility or telecom to move service from an old address to a new one — landing the shutoff date and start date so the user is never billed for two premises at once and never goes dark on day-of-move.
disclaimer: Some services (fiber, water with deposits) require physical work at the new address before service starts. Build in a 24-48 hour buffer at the NEW end so a missed appointment doesn't leave the user without service; never put the buffer on the OLD end (overlap charges).

context.when_to_use: User is moving and wants to transfer the same provider's service to the new address. NOT for switching providers (use cancellation skills for that) and NOT for adding a second service to an existing account.
preconditions: Move-out date at old address (confirmed lease end or closing date).
Move-in date at new address (when keys are received / closing).
New address with unit number and ZIP (verify service is available there before calling).
Whether the new address requires any equipment swap (different cable infrastructure, different gas type, well vs. municipal water).

principles: Set the shutoff at the OLD address for the day AFTER move-out — never the same day. Movers can run late; a same-day shutoff means cleaning crews and walk-throughs in the dark.
Set the start at the NEW address for the day BEFORE move-in — never the same day. You want power and water to be on when the truck arrives, not 'scheduled for activation between 8am-8pm'.
Result: 1-2 days of overlap is FINE and worth a few dollars; 0 days of overlap is the trap that creates the all-day shutoff-day phone call.
If the rep books this as a 'service transfer' rather than 'disconnect old + connect new', the account number carries over and avoids a new deposit. Insist on transfer wording.
Recognise wrong-queue: 'new connects' and 'disconnects' are sometimes separate teams from 'moves'. If routed wrong, request a warm transfer that preserves your hold position.

phrases.opener: Hi, I'm moving and I want to transfer service from [old address] to [new address]. Move-out is [date], move-in is [date]. I'd like to set this up as a transfer on the same account, not a disconnect-plus-new-connect.
phrases.queue_check: Just to make sure I'm in the right place — is this the moves team, or are disconnects and new connects separate teams? I want to handle both in one call.
phrases.transfer_request: Could you do a warm transfer to the moves team and stay on the line until they pick up? I'd rather not lose my hold position.
phrases.request_transfer_not_new: Can we set this up as a service transfer — same account number, no new deposit, no new equipment installation? My understanding is that triggers a different workflow than a disconnect.
phrases.stage_dates: I'd like the shutoff at the old address scheduled for [move-out date + 1] — day AFTER move-out — and the new address activated on [move-in date - 1] — day BEFORE move-in. I'm okay with one or two days of overlap.
phrases.check_service_availability: Before we lock this in — can you confirm the new address is in your service area, what infrastructure is there [coax / fiber / DSL / electric panel size if known], and whether there's any pre-install requirement?
phrases.ask_truck_roll: Will a tech need to come out to the new address? If yes, what's the earliest appointment, what's the window, and is there a fee?
phrases.ask_equipment: Does my current equipment work at the new address, or do I need a swap? If a swap, can it be mailed ahead so I'm not waiting on a tech day-of?
phrases.ask_promo_preservation: I'm on a [promo / loyalty rate]. Will that carry over to the new address, or does this trigger a re-rate? If it re-rates, I'd like that flagged before we proceed.
phrases.lock_in_writing: Can you email or text the confirmation with both dates, both addresses, account number, any new charges, and any appointment windows? I want it in writing before we hang up.
phrases.graceful_close: Thanks — so shutoff at old on [date], activation at new on [date], same account, no new deposit, promo carries, tech appointment [date / window], confirmation number [number].

tactics.name: Stage the dates correctly
tactics.script: Use `stage_dates`. Phrase it explicitly as 'day after move-out' / 'day before move-in' so the rep cannot mis-key it as same-day.

tactics.name: Insist on 'transfer' not 'disconnect + new'
tactics.when: Rep starts opening two work orders.
tactics.script: Use `request_transfer_not_new`. Same-account transfer skips new-customer credit check, deposit, and often promo loss.

tactics.name: Recognise wrong-queue / cross-team routing
tactics.when: Rep can only do one side (disconnect OR new-connect).
tactics.script: Use `queue_check` and `transfer_request`. Handling both in one call avoids the gap where one side gets done and the other never gets scheduled.

tactics.name: Verify service availability at new address
tactics.script: Use `check_service_availability`. Especially critical for fiber, gigabit cable, or 5G fixed-wireless — 'available' on the marketing map sometimes doesn't match the actual install database.

tactics.name: Pin down truck roll + equipment logistics
tactics.script: Use `ask_truck_roll` and `ask_equipment`. A 4-hour install window on move-in day is the most common cause of move-day chaos.

tactics.name: Protect any existing promo / discount
tactics.script: Use `ask_promo_preservation`. Many promos are address-locked; transferring sometimes re-rates to current pricing. Flag it before the move, not after the first bill.

tactics.name: Lock it in writing
tactics.script: Use `lock_in_writing`. Move-related verbal arrangements are the #1 source of billing disputes.

boundaries: Do NOT schedule shutoff at the old address before the move-out date — even one day early can mean no water during the move-out clean.
Do NOT schedule activation at the new address after the move-in date — even one day late can mean no power for the first night.
Do NOT accept a new account number without explicit reason. New account = new deposit + new credit check + lost promo, in most cases.
Do NOT skip confirming equipment compatibility — showing up with the wrong modem on day one is a 3-day delay.
success_signals: Both dates scheduled with 1-2 day buffer in the correct direction.
Same account number carries over, no new deposit.
Promo / loyalty rate confirmed to carry.
Written confirmation with both dates, both addresses, and appointment window.
Equipment plan confirmed (carry it / mail-ahead / day-of swap).
failure_signals: Rep insists on disconnect-then-new-connect with new deposit.
Promo silently re-rates to current pricing and rep won't escalate.
Truck-roll appointment is more than 1 week after requested activation date.
Service not actually available at new address despite map claim.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm both dates, both addresses, account, deposit status, promo status, equipment plan, and appointment window. Calendar both dates with 24-hour reminders.
exit_strategy.on_failure: If promo will be lost, evaluate switching providers at the new address (often cheaper than transfer with re-rate). If service is not available at new address, get the unavailable-area documentation in writing — you may need it to break a contract without ETF.
follow_ups: Day before old-address shutoff: confirm via portal that the work order is still scheduled.
Day before new-address activation: confirm appointment time and tech ETA.
Day after activation: verify the new account looks right and old account shows final-bill status.
Two weeks after: verify the final bill at old address is reasonable (no phantom recurring charges).

required_user_info: New address (full, with unit) and move-in date
